tiles.answer: {"Theorem 1": "H and gamma are asymptotically independent", "Theorem 2": "H(C) ~ log(eff_rank(X)) / log(n)", "Theorem 3": "P48 preserves H with epsilon tolerance", "Conjecture 4": "Phase transition at gamma_c = 1/(n-1)"}

tiles.question: Corrected Theorem 1: H-gamma tradeoff (2026-05-15)
tiles.answer: {"Theorem1_Corrected": "For fixed V, rho(gamma, H) \u2248 -0.5 (NEGATIVE). Connectivity and diversity TRADE OFF against each other in coupling matrices. This is a fundamental resource constraint: more edges (higher gamma) reduce spectral entropy (lower H). The 4-regime phase space is a PARETO FRONTIER where Regime III (high both) is the optimal balance.", "Scaling": "p_crit \u2248 2V^(-0.8) for gamma > 0.15 emergence threshold", "V_baselines": {"V=3": "gamma=0.513\u00b10.280 H=0.992\u00b10.007", "V=4": "gamma=0.391\u00b10.194 H=0.989\u00b10.006", "V=10": "gamma=0.175\u00b10.085 H=0.982\u00b10.004", "V=30": "gamma=0.088\u00b10.047 H=0.961\u00b10.002", "V=100": "gamma=0.044\u00b10.026 H=0.901\u00b10.002"}}

tiles.question: theorem/conservation-law
tiles.answer: {"theorem": "Conservation Law", "formula": "gamma + H = 1.364 - 0.159*log(V)", "r_squared": 0.9956, "valid_range": {"V_min": 3, "V_max": 100}, "evidence": "Empirically verified across V=3..100 with 1000+ samples", "caveat": "TYPE-DEPENDENT \u2014 style coupling vs topology coupling give different constants", "fleet_math_function": "coupling_entropy(), algebraic_normalized() in fleet_math.health", "source_session": "2026-05-15 100-turn session"}

tiles.question: theorem/canonical-decomposition
tiles.answer: {"theorem": "Canonical Decomposition", "statement": "Fleet state = Topology \u00d7 Style \u00d7 Timing. Three independent manifolds.", "evidence": "r(gamma_topo, H_style) = 0.013 (p=0.818)", "sub_theorems": ["gamma controlled by edge density p: gamma(p) = 0.791*p^1.042, R\u00b2=0.990", "H controlled by latent rank k: H(k) = 1 - 0.716*exp(-0.057*k), R\u00b2=0.973", "gamma(H) = 0.879*exp(-2.906*H) when computed from same matrix"], "implication": "gamma and H are independent structures. The tradeoff (rho=-0.5) is a MATRIX PROJECTION ARTIFACT \u2014 two independent structures correlated by measurement lens.", "source_session": "2026-05-15 Batch 2 Heavy Clean test"}

tiles.question: theorem/h-gamma-tradeoff
tiles.answer: {"theorem": "H-gamma Pareto Tradeoff", "statement": "When computed from the SAME coupling matrix, gamma and H negatively correlate at rho \u2248 -0.5 for fixed V.", "mechanism": "Resource constraint in the coupling matrix \u2014 connectivity and diversity compete for the same spectral budget", "regime_III": "Emergence requires gamma > gamma_c AND H > 1/phi simultaneously \u2014 the Pareto-efficient frontier", "note": "This is not fundamental \u2014 it's a property of the MATRIX, not the FLEET. Separate channels break the tradeoff.", "source_session": "2026-05-15"}

tiles.question: theorem/p48-invariance
tiles.answer: {"theorem": "P48 (Pythagorean48) is lossless for spectral monitoring", "delta": "< 0.01% change in H-gamma after P48 quantization", "validated_across": "V=3 through V=100", "practical": "P48 encoding preserves spectral structure. Fleet health monitoring works identically on quantized and raw style vectors."}
